summary: Founding engineer turned Director of Engineering at Meridian Logistics, where I grew the team from just myself to 20+ engineers spanning backend, frontend, QA, DevOps, and release management, and delivered the infrastructure behind Meridian Logistics becoming the first regulated fintech platform in the US. I architected the backend systems and processes the platform is built on, took releases from ad hoc to a structured bi-weekly cadence across 4 engineering teams, and led the engineering and compliance work across a 7-year regulatory build-out. Hands-on with Node.js and TypeScript, and equally comfortable shaping technical strategy with C-suite leadership, I'm targeting Director of Engineering and VP of Engineering roles where team development and delivery excellence are priorities.

bullets.text: Grew the engineering organization from sole backend engineer to a 20+ person team across backend, frontend, QA, DevOps, and release management, with full hiring authority and ownership of every career-level and cultural decision.

Architected the microservices backend framework and PostgreSQL database schemas that Meridian Logistics's platform is built on today, containerizing services and automating deployments with GitHub Actions and CI/CD pipelines to support a regulated platform that scaled from a single engineer to a 20+ person team across backend, DevOps, and QA.

Standardized release management across 4 engineering teams, replacing ad hoc deployments with a structured bi-weekly cadence 3x faster than the prior process, delivering consistent predictability across 5+ regulated product milestones including regulatory the platform approval, custody launch, ETH custody, and clearing and settlement approval.

Embedded technical debt cleanup as a standing sprint commitment, keeping a codebase that grew from 1 to 20+ engineers stable in a regulatory-regulated environment where shortcuts carried real compliance risk.

Wrote engineering standards and code review processes from scratch as the founding engineer, prioritizing long-term maintainability across a codebase and team that grew from 1 to 20+ engineers under regulatory regulation.

Piloted AI-assisted development and QA workflows using Claude and Cursor across the 20+ person team, shifting both software and QA engineers to AI-native tooling and establishing the working patterns the team adopted going forward.

Held a dual engineering and product IC role — defining requirements with stakeholders and contributing to architecture decisions while establishing the QA standards, CI/CD pipelines, and PR annotation practices that governed how the team designed, reviewed, and shipped code.

Established QA standards and testing protocols with coverage verification via Codecov, CI/CD pipelines, and Xray scripts linked to Jira stories, taking test coverage from roughly 50% to 100% as part of the peer review gates.

Introduced a PR annotation practice requiring engineers to comment on their own code with explanations of logic and intent, reducing bugs and tech debt through self-review and cutting time from in-progress to code-complete by 20%.
Built Docker automation that cut engineer onboarding time from several weeks to a few days.
Built internal tooling in JavaScript, React.js, HTML, and CSS within an Agile workflow, contributing to automation initiatives that reduced engineer onboarding from over two weeks to three days.

Built the frontend media player and user platform in AngularJS, styled with LessCSS and bundled with Grunt, backed by a Node.js/Express API handling cloud storage interactions for user media collections.
Migrated the platform from a PHP backend to a Node.js/MEAN stack.

Led engineering on mid-sized client projects, managing timelines, delivery goals, and direct client communication to define requirements and produce action plans for custom applications including accounting and reservation software.
Managed end-to-end delivery of custom client applications by running weekly stakeholder check-ins, defining requirements, and tracking work velocity in Pivotal Tracker to keep timelines on target.
